channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: NBA The Run Is Getting A Single-Player Campaign Mode
item.content:encoded:
Since its launch earlier this month, NBA The Run has had millions of games played on the various courts around the world. But there’s been one thing that many players have questioned with the game: why is there no single-player mode?

The game is built around online play and teaming up with others. Sometimes, though, you just want to play a basketball game without dealing with anyone else. Developer Play by Play Studios has heard that feedback, and, on Thursday, announced its plans to do something about it with a new single-player Campaign Mode for NBA The Run.

A Campaign Done “Right”

There was no mention of what the new campaign mode would entail. All that was confirmed was that it will be able to be played solo or with friends, both locally and online.

The mode is still in the concept stage, so there isn’t much else to share at the moment. Play by Play Studios says that they want to do this mode right by the players. To do that, they said, it’s going to take time. And right now, they are looking at a Spring 2027 release date for the mode.

More Content Is Coming

In the meantime, NBA The Run is getting more content for players. New players are set to come in August, along with a new camera angle, practice mode, new rulesets, new courts, and leaderboards.

Also planned for later this year is a Ranked/Ladder mode as well as voice chat. More details on specific timelines for those will come at a later date.

The largest update so far for the game comes in August with the start of Season 1. You can also check out the full roadmap below.

What do you think of the planned updates, including a Campaign Mode, for NBA The Run? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about Deltarune hitting a new Steam player count milestone with Chapter 5. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag Resynced Had 15 Ubisoft Studios Developing It
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming has learned about the stark amount of resources that Ubisoft has put into Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag Resynced.

Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag Resynced is scheduled to launch in a matter of weeks, on July 9, 2026, and Ubisoft has put a massive amount of resources into its development. While the exact number of developers is currently unknown, 15 studios at Ubisoft had involvement, including some who recently saw layoffs and closures.

15 Studios Worked on Black Flag Resynced

Insider Gaming understands that the following studios worked on Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced:

  • Singapore (lead studio)
  • Barcelona
  • Belgrade
  • Bordeaux
  • Bucharest
  • Chengdu
  • DaNang
  • India
  • Kyiv
  • Montpellier
  • Montreal
  • Philippines
  • Shanghai
  • Sofia
  • Quebec

Of the 15 studios, Ubisoft has since shut down the Belgrade studio as part of the publisher’s recent cost restructuring efforts, which also saw Winnipeg close its doors and Barcelona undergo changes, including layoffs.

Assassin’s Creed: Shadows also had a similar number of studios working on it, but the budgets of both games are currently unknown.

Assassin’s Creed Blag Flag Resynced will launch on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series S|X, and PC on July 9, 2026.

RELATED – Leaked Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced DLC Pack Looks Like Dark Souls

Are you looking forward to playing Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about the latest on the pending closure of Compulsion Games at Xbox. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1833: Hints and Answer for June 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Wordle challenges players to guess a fresh five-letter word each day in six tries or fewer. Whether you’re a beginner or a daily player, today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1833 for June 26, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, June 26, 2026

Today’s Wordle answer for game #1833 is a somewhat challenging puzzle. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 3.6 out of 6 guesses, which is slightly lower than the 3.8-guess average for June 25.

General Wordle Hints For June 26

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: It describes something sharp or severe rather than gradual or mild.
  • Hint 2: The word is commonly used in medicine, geometry, and everyday conversation.
  • Hint 3: In geometry, it can describe a certain type of angle.
  • Hint 4: It often refers to conditions, pain, or situations that are intense but relatively short-term.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1833 for June 26, 2026.

Starter Words For June 26, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • CAUSE
  • OUTER
  • TRACE
  • SAUCE
  • CRATE

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1833 On June 26, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: ACUTE

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

ACUTE is an adjective that means “sharp or severe in effect” or “extremely sensitive even to slight details or impressions.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1832 on June 25, 2026

Answer: UNITY

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1833

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads-up. The game on June 27 will be Wordle Game #1834.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Escape from Tarkov Head Reveals Decade-Old Throwback Plans
item.content:encoded:
Escape from Tarkov is a legendary extraction shooter that came together over a decade of development. It’s still evolving today, and in November 2025, it transitioned out of ‘beta’, becoming a 1.0 game after many years of work. It’s still not perfect, but the team at Battlestate Games is determined to make it the best extraction game on the market.

Recently, Nikita Buyanov, the head of the studio at Battlestate Games, took to social media (as he often does) to share a throwback image from 2015. It was his ‘first plan’ for Escape from Tarkov, and it featured a map system that’s scarily close to what we have today, all these years later.

Ten Years of Evolution, Still Pretty Close

On social media, Nikita Buyanov, the prolific personality who often fronts Escape from Tarkov, posted an image from December 2015. He declared it his first plan for Escape from Tarkov, an extraction shooter that is weathering all comers and proving why it’s the oldest name in the space.

In the image, we see a rough sketch of what would eventually become Escape from Tarkov’s map system, including almost all the locations we have today. It’s laid out pretty similarly to what we have right now in the game, with a few tweaks and ‘size changes’ for the maps.

It shows a progressive system, presumably with the player starting at the first point and making their way through every map in succession before reaching the final map, a port. This effectively mirrors the game as it is now, with players being invited to ‘escape from Tarkov’ by heading to that final map, now called Terminal.

The map starts in ‘City’ and then works through nine further locations:

  • Suburbs
  • Interchange
  • Customs
  • Factory
  • Woods
  • VIllage
  • Lighthouse
  • Harbor
  • Port

Along the way, there are side locations, such as Private Sector, Laboratory, Base, and Basements. We can also see some ‘Arena’ locations dotted around the map, which would eventually become Escape from Tarkov: Arena, a spin-off shooter launched in December 2023.

  • RELATED: Latest Escape from Tarkov Roadmap

It’s a phenomenal blast from the past and a testament to how true Buyanov stayed to his original vision for Escape from Tarkov, after all these years.

Have you ever given Escape from Tarkov a try? Let us know what you think about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the Resident Evil and Monster Hunter collab, and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming Newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Latest on GTA 6 Collector’s Edition—Expected Release & Contents
item.content:encoded:
Everyone is expecting a Grand Theft Auto 6 Collector’s Edition because Rockstar Games has a history of doing them. It’s the best and most complete way to celebrate GTA 6, and let’s check out the latest.

GTA 6 is going to sell a bazillion copies when all is said and done. $80, $100, $1,000, slightly hyperbolic, it doesn’t matter, people have been waiting over a decade for a new GTA game.

Rockstar Games only delivers perfection, and they’ve been working hard to deliver the best content and editions, which brings us to a possible Collector’s Edition.

Is GTA 6 Getting a Collector’s Edition?

Right now, it’s unconfirmed whether GTA 6 will receive a traditional Collector’s Edition, as many Rockstar games have received.

Firstly, this is very much an ‘in-progress’ guide, as we are live tracking anything about a potential Collector’s Edition.

Unless Rockstar Games has had a big change of heart or is pursuing a different business direction, it makes complete financial sense to do a GTA 6 Collector’s Edition.

  • More Insider Gaming: All GTA 6 Editions, Prices, Content Included & Pre-Order Bonuses

GTA 5, GTA 4, and Red Dead Redemption 2 all had a Collector’s Edition.

It doesn’t matter how many Rockstar makes or how much they cost, GTA 6 Collector’s Editions would sell out. It would be bizarre if there wasn’t one ready to be revealed at some point.

When Could GTA 6 Collector’s Edition be Announced?

If a GTA 6 Collector’s Edition happens, it should still be before the game’s full launch and follow the lead of Rockstar titles.

One thing that concerns me slightly is that Rockstar announced Red Dead Redemption 2’s Special Edition, Ultimate Edition, and Collector’s Box together—the same with Grand Theft Auto V Special Edition and Collector’s Edition.

  • More Insider Gaming: All GTA 6 PlayStation-Exclusive Features—Every PS5 & PS5 Pro Bonus

It’s breaking the trend slightly by not announcing a GTA 6 Collector’s Edition alongside the Base and Ultimate Editions. However, it doesn’t mean we should rule it out.

We have seen a retailer list GTA 6 Collector’s Edition and a price, but this could be a placeholder.

What Could GTA 6 Collector’s Edition Include?

GTA 6 Collector’s Edition could feature anything from a map poster to GTA 6 memorabilia like clothes, keyrings, collectible figures, and a statue.

The GTA 4 Special Edition (Collector’s Edition) came in a giant cover art box, and included the game, a giant security lock GTA 4 tin, an art book, a soundtrack, a keyring, and more.

  • More Insider Gaming: Will GTA 6 be on Disc? Physical Edition Pre-Order Guide

Whereas GTA 5’s Collector’s Edition featured a map, steelbook, a fun money bag, and other goodies.

However, the Red Dead Redemption 2 Collector’s Edition is a red flag, as it didn’t actually come with the game and was sold separately!

Time will tell if the expected Collector’s Edition happens, how much Rockstar charges, and if the game will be included.

Would you be tempted by a Collector’s Edition? Let us know through the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more GTA guides, check out how to claim a free month of GTA+ with GTA 6, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Apple Raises Prices On MacBooks and iPads By Hundreds Of Dollars, Blames Memory Shortage
item.content:encoded:
Apple is joining the list of companies raising the prices of their devices, and blaming it on memory and component shortages.

On Thursday, Apple’s online store revealed price increases across several product lineups, including iPads and MacBooks, among other devices. These price increases range from $30 to as much as $1,300, and are effective immediately.

MacBooks, iPads, and Mac Studios All Jump in Price

At least 15 devices have seen their prices increase, as first reported by Macrumors. The most modest increase comes on the HomePod mini, going from $99 to $129. On the other end of the spectrum, you have the Mac Studio M3 Ultra jumping $1,300 from $3,999 to $5,299.

You can check out all of the big changes below, courtesy of Macrumors.

  • HomePod mini: $129 from $99
  • HomePod: $349 from $299
  • Apple TV: $199 from $129
  • iPad: $449 from $349
  • iPad mini: $599 from $499
  • iPad Air: $749 from $599
  • iPad Pro: $1,199 from $999
  • MacBook Neo: $699 from $599
  • MacBook Air: $1,299 from $1,099
  • MacBook Pro: $1,999 from $1,699
  • iMac: $1,499 from $1,299
  • Mac mini (M4 Pro): $1,599 from $1,399
  • Mac Studio (M4 Max): $2,499 from $1,999
  • Mac Studio (M3 Ultra): $5,299 from $3,999
  • Vision Pro: $3,699 from $3,499

These Increases Were Expected

Apple raising its prices shouldn’t come as a surprise. Despite many viewing it as unnecessary, Apple CEO Tim Cook recently said in an interview with The Wall Street Journal that it was going to happen.

“Unfortunately, price increases are unavoidable,” Cook told the outlet. “We’re doing our best to mitigate the huge increases that are being passed to us, and we’ve been trying to shield our customers from the increases, but the situation has become unsustainable.”

What do you think of Apple raising its prices on MacBooks, iPads, and more? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about the latest on the pending closure of Compulsion Games at Xbox. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: G2 Bench SunPayus Despite IEM Cologne 2026 Major Run; Sign r1nkle
item.content:encoded:
G2 has announced the benching of Spanish AWPer Álvaro ‘SunPayus⁠’ García. The announcement comes after G2 made the quarter-finals of the IEM Cologne 2026 Major, finishing 5th-8th in the biggest competition of the Counter-Strike calendar.

Soon after, G2 announced the signing of Artem ‘r1nkle’ Moroz. The 21-year-old Ukrainian AWPer was on the Ninjas in Pyjamas bench after the team failed to qualify for the Major.

G2 remove SunPayus; Sign r1nkle

Despite the relatively positive finish to the season, G2 has decided to remove SunPayus with r1nkle taking his place in the new G2 roster.

G2’s run at IEM Cologne saw the team impressively make the playoffs, defeating NAVI to secure a playoff berth and playing out a classic series against Team Spirit in the quarter-finals to narrowly fall out of the competition.

While the season ended relatively positively, G2 spent much of the season far away from a sniff of a trophy, last lifting silverware at BLAST Open London 2025 in September of last year.

G2 have then opted for a gamble, replacing SunPayus with benched 21-year-old NiP AWPer, r1nkle. r1nkle’s aggressive playstyle has proved to be extremely inconsistent in his time at NiP, at times their redemption and at times their doom, costing NiP dearly in important rounds.

G2 will be hoping they can polish a rough diamond, but there is an obvious risk attached to the gambit. If r1nkle cannot sand off his rough edges, G2 may be left wishing they had SunPayus’ reliability back, even if he had a lower ceiling than the talented Ukrainian.

  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

What does the SunPayus benching tell us about G2?

The SunPayus benching is an enlightening direction for the roster, considering the internal dynamics of the team and its current direction.

We know that Eetu ‘sAw’ Saha had previously worked with SunPayus at ENCE, as had Guy ‘NertZ’ Iluz. Following NertZ’s addition to G2, it had seemed at first that it was a happy reunion for all involved, but it has proven to be a short one.

For SunPayus, on an individual level, that ENCE stay remains the pinnacle of his individual performance level, suffering a severe downturn in Counter-Strike 2 in comparison to his CSGO days.

Nevertheless, G2 had potential reasons to keep him around, partially because of his established relationships with the ENCE cohort, but also because of his voice in-game.

SunPayus could frequently be seen doing a large portion of the mid-round communication for G2’s CT-sides, specifically, and without him, they are losing that voice. SunPayus had also acted as IGL for G2 at IEM Rio, where G2 were missing Nemanja ‘huNter-‘ Kovač.

For G2, it may be that having that strong voice was not in the end what was desired in the roster, with huNter- establishing himself as more in control of comms after the SunPayus benching.

Certainly, r1nkle will prove to be more of a junior partner in the G2 project, with the 21-year-old playing more individually for his own stats rather than commanding G2’s troops.

Let us know your thoughts about the move on the Insider Gaming Discord


For other news, see Gentle Mates leaving Counter-Strike and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PlayStation’s Hermen Hulst Calls Bungie Layoffs “Necessary” In Memo To Staff
item.content:encoded:
On Thursday, Bungie announced layoffs at the studio. Or, as they called it, a “reduction in force”. Following the announcement, however, a memo sent out by PlayStation CEO Hermen Hulst was published by PlayStation regarding the layoffs at Bungie.

In the memo, Hulst confirms that “most of the Destiny team and some Marathon team members” were let go. It was also said there were layoffs across Sony teams that support Bungie. An exact number, however, was not provided.

Hulst’s Full Memo On The Bungie Layoffs

In his message to the company, Hulst confirmed which teams were impacted by the layoffs at Bungie. He also called the news “painful” and said it was done after “extensive discussion and careful consideration”. He also said they were “necessary to align the studio’s resources with its current priorities and long-term goals.”

You can read Hulst’s full memo to the staff below, courtesy of PlayStation:

Dear Team, 

Today I want to share a difficult update regarding Bungie. 

We have made the decision to reduce Bungie’s workforce, affecting a significant number of employees, including most of the Destiny team and some Marathon team members. There are also reductions across SIE teams that support Bungie’s operations. Those impacted at Bungie and within SIE are being informed today. 

This is painful news, especially for talented colleagues whose roles have been eliminated. This decision was made only after extensive discussion and careful consideration, and I want to provide some context on how we arrived here. Over the past several months, together with Bungie leadership, we reviewed the studio’s long-term direction, development priorities, resource needs, and role within our broader portfolio strategy. We explored multiple alternatives before concluding that a reduction was necessary to align the studio’s resources with its current priorities and long-term goals. 

As Bungie recently shared in its Destiny 2 update, the studio has begun a new journey following the release of the game’s final live-service content update. What Bungie has accomplished with Destiny over the past decade has been truly remarkable. The franchise has left a lasting mark on players and the industry, and everyone who contributed to its success should be proud of what they helped create. 

Marathon remains an important part of our portfolio, and we will continue to support the team as they build on the strong foundation established in Season 1 and 2, and as they work on incubation efforts for future projects. While it’s too early to discuss, we are encouraged by the creativity and opportunities that lie ahead. 

Our immediate priority is supporting affected employees through this transition. We are providing transition support and, where possible, working to identify opportunities across SIE and our global network of studios. 

I want to sincerely thank every affected employee for their hard work, creativity, and contributions to Bungie, SIE, and the broader gaming community. 

I know today’s news is deeply difficult not only for those leaving, but for those colleagues and friends that remain. Please take the time you need to process this news and support one another.  

Thank you for your resilience and continued support during this difficult time. 

Hermen Hulst 
CEO, Studio Business Group 

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw
